content.thinking: Let me look at the chart image first.

content.text:
<think>
The chart shows summer temperature distributions for two periods: 1951-1980 (baseline) and 2013-2023.

Comparing the two distributions:
- In 1951-1980, the distribution is centered around "Normal" with significant portions in "Cold" and "Hot" categories
- In 2013-2023, the distribution has shifted to the right (warmer), meaning more "Hot" and "Extremely hot" and less "Cold" and "Normal"

Looking at what we see less of in recent years (2013-2023 vs the baseline):
- "Cold" - the blue section is clearly much smaller/reduced in the 2013-2023 chart
- "Normal" - the gray/white section appears smaller too
- "Hot" - appears larger in recent years
- "Extremely hot" - appears larger in recent years

So in recent years, we're seeing less "cold" and "normal" weather. The question asks about "cold, normal, hot, extremely hot" - so the answer would be "cold" and "normal".
</think>

<answer>
Cold and normal. In the 2013-2023 distribution, the curve has shifted sharply rightward compared to the 1951-1980 baseline. The blue ("Cold") and gray ("Normal") sections are significantly smaller, while "Hot" and "Extremely hot" have grown substantially.
</answer>
